zh.lifeSummary: 當Effie Leona White於1932年10月2日出生在美國華盛頓州斯卡馬尼亞卡森時，她的父親Roy Captain White30歲，母親Ruth Mary Covey30歲。她於1959年5月9日與Orville Oscar Sullivan在美國華盛頓州斯卡馬尼亞卡森結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於2015年5月13日在美國華盛頓州克拉克溫哥華享年82歲，埋葬在美國華盛頓州克拉克溫哥華艾福格林 梅莫利亞爾 加登斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Effie Leona White于1932年10月2日出生在美国华盛顿州斯卡馬尼亞卡森时，她的父亲Roy Captain White30岁，母亲Ruth Mary Covey30岁。她于1959年5月9日与Orville Oscar Sullivan在美国华盛顿州斯卡馬尼亞卡森结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于2015年5月13日在美国华盛顿州克拉克溫哥華享年82岁，埋葬在美国华盛顿州克拉克溫哥華艾福格林 梅莫利亞爾 加登斯。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Effie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a girl's name it is a pet form of   Euphemia   or   Hephzibah   (now rarely used in their full forms, but popular in the 19th century) and is occasionally bestowed independently or as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Oighrig . As a boy's name it is a pet form of   Ephraim .

nameMeanings.name: Leona
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinate feminine form of   Leon .

historicEvents.title: The FBI is Established
historicEvents.description: The Bureau of Investigation's name was changed to the Federal Bureau of Investigation to help citizens know that the Government is helping protect from threats both domestically and abroad.

Galloping Gertie is the reference used to describe the Tacoma Narrows Bridge. It opened on July 1, 1940 four months later it no longer existed. On November 7, 1940 the wind gusts came up to 40 miles an hour causing the bridge to twist and vibrate violently before it collapsed into Puget Sound. The only victim of the bridge collapsing was a three-legged paralyzed dog named Tubby whose owner tried to rescue him from the car but he wouldn’t go with him.

historicEvents.title: The Civil Rights Movement Begins
historicEvents.description: The civil rights movement was a movement to enforce constitutional and legal rights for African Americans that the other Americans enjoyed. By using nonviolent campaigns, those involved secured new recognition in laws and federal protection of all Americans. Moderators worked with Congress to pass of several pieces of legislation that overturned discriminatory practices.

historicEvents.title: Seattle Space Needle is Built
historicEvents.description: Construction on the Seattle Space Needle was constructed on April 17, 1961, and was opened on April 21, 1962. It was built for the World’s Fair in 1962. The space needle is an observation tower that is iconic to Seattle and a landmark of the Pacific Northwest. It was meant to only be lite for 75 nights per year or less to reduce light pollution. On September 11, 2001 the lights of the space were left on for 11 days straight.

historicEvents.title: The Copyright Act of 1976
historicEvents.description: The primary form of copyright law in the United States. The Act spells out what is the basic rights of the copyright holder and what is classified as fair use.

Dedicated on November 17-21, 1980 by President Spencer W. Kimball, the Seattle Washington Temple for the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints was the first temple built in the Pacific Northwest and in Washington. It is also only one of five temples where the angel Moroni is holding the gold plates.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Temples of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints- Seattle Washington Temple

historicEvents.description: Atlanta, Georgia, United States hosts Summer Olympic Games.

historicEvents.title: Spokane Washington Temple
historicEvents.description: Dedicated on August 21-23,1999 by President Gordon B. Hinckley the Spokane Washington Temple was the second built in Washington. The oxen for the font and angel Moroni were delivered by a trucker from Albuquerque, New Mexico.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Temples of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints- Spokane Washington Temple

en.lifeSummary: When Effie Leona White was born on 2 October 1932, in Carson, Skamania, Washington, United States, her father, Roy Captain White, was 30 and her mother, Ruth Mary Covey, was 30. She married Orville Oscar Sullivan on 9 May 1959, in Carson, Skamania, Washington, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 13 May 2015, in Vancouver, Clark, Washington, United States, at the age of 82, and was buried in Evergreen Memorial Gardens, Vancouver, Clark, Washington, United States.

Elle a épousé Orville Oscar Sullivan le 9 mai 1959, à Carson, Skamania, Washington, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. ja.lifeSummary: Effie Leona Whiteが1932年10月2日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州スカマニアカーソンで生まれた時，父Roy Captain Whiteは30歳，母Ruth Mary Coveyは30歳であった。1959年5月9日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州スカマニアカーソンでOrville Oscar Sullivanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は2015年5月13日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クラークヴァンコウバーで82歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クラークヴァンコウバーエバーグレン メモーイアル ガーデンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Effie Leona White(이)가 1932년 10월 2일 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roy Captain White(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ruth Mary Covey(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1959년 5월 9일 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨에서 Orville Oscar Sullivan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 2015년 5월 13일 미국 워싱턴 주 클라르크 버코우버에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 워싱턴 주 클라르크 버코우버 에버그린 모리앨 가덴스에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Orville Oscar Sullivan em 9 de maio de 1959, em Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.Evergreen Memorial Gardens, Vancouver, C: эвиргрин меморяль гарденс, ванкоувир, кларк, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты
ru.White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Uni: уит салмон, кликкитат, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Effie Leona White родилась 2 октября 1932 г. в местности карсон, скаманя, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Roy Captain White было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ruth Mary Covey, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Orville Oscar Sullivan 9 мая 1959 г. в местности карсон, скаманя, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 13 мая 2015 г. в местности ванкоувир, кларк, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности эвиргрин меморяль гарденс, ванкоувир, кларк, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Effie Leona White nació el 2 de octubre de 1932, en Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos, su papá, Roy Captain White, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ruth Mary Covey, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Orville Oscar Sullivan el 9 de mayo de 1959, en Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 13 de mayo de 2015, en Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Evergreen Memorial Gardens, Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos.
